zh.lifeSummary: 當John Leche - Esquire of Eggemere and Wigtown, Norfolk於1255年出生在英國英格蘭德比夏爾德比時，他的父親Edmund Le Leche25歲，母親Mrs. Margaret Le Leche10歲。他於1275年之前與Cecilla de Dunbar結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1370年之後在英國英格蘭爾切斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Leche - Esquire of Eggemere and Wigtown, Norfolk于1255年出生在英国英格兰德比夏爾德比时，他的父亲Edmund Le Leche25岁，母亲Mrs. Margaret Le Leche10岁。他于1275年之前与Cecilla de Dunbar结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1370年之后在英国英格兰爾切斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Leche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: variant of   Letch   or   Leech  .

variants.name: Letch
meanings: English: locative name, either topographic from Middle English lech(e), lecche, lache ‘sluggish stream, wet place’ (Old English * læcc(e), * lecc(e) ‘stream’), or toponymic from a place named with this word, such as Lach Dennis and Lache (both Cheshire) and Eastleach and Northleach (both Gloucs).

variants.name: Leech
meanings: English: variant of   Leach  .
Irish (Galway):English name adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó MaolMhaodhóg (see Logue ).

nameMeanings.name: Esquire
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name, occupational name, or a nickname from Middle English squyer, squer, skier, sker, northern Middle English swire (Old French escuyer, escuier) ‘shield bearer, esquire, personal attendant to a knight; landowner below the rank of knight; young man of gentle birth; household attendant, retainer, page, messenger’. Many early bearers of this name were of modest means and cannot have been members of the gentry; in such cases the name may have been used hyperbolically for a low-ranking servant or as a nickname. By the 17th century, the term denoted any member of the landed gentry, but this is unlikely to have influenced the development of the surname.

variants.name: Swire
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of   Squire  , from a northern form of the word.

variants.name: Quire
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of   Squire  .
Scottish or Irish: shortened form of   McQuire   (see   McGuire  ).

variants.name: Squires
meanings: English: variant of Squire , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Scuderi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmelo, Salvatore, Angelo, Carlo, Gaetano, Vito, Antonio, Dante, Mario, Rocco, Rosario, Antonino, Biaggio, Eduardo, Ermelinda, Margarita.

Italian (Sicily): status name equivalent to Squire , from scudiero, scutiero, dialect scuteri ‘shieldbearer’ (from medieval Latin scutarius, an agent derivative of scutum ‘shield’). In Byzantium, the Greek word skoutarios denoted a member of the imperial guard.

variants.name: Square
meanings: English (Devon) and Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Squire  .

variants.name: Squier
meanings: English: variant of   Squire  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Canadian Hénault or directly of French Hunault (see Henault 2).
Catalan (Añó): Castilianized form of Anyó, probably from the old personal name Annone.
Japanese: variant of   Anno  .

variants.name: Anno
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Masayuki, Tadashi, Yoshito.
Japanese: written 安野 ‘peaceful plain’. This surname is rare in Japan proper, and may have originated in the Ryūkyū Islands, where it is pronounced   Ano  .

variants.name: Henault
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Lucien, Martial, Pierre, Camille, Fernand, Laurier, Monique, Normand, Philippe.
French (Hénault): from the ancient Germanic personal name Haginwald, composed of the elements hagin ‘enclosure, protected place’ and wald ‘power, authority’.
Altered form (Hénault) of French Hunault: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hunwald, composed of the elements hun ‘bear cub’ and wald ‘power, authority’. Compare Ano and Eno .

nameMeanings.name: Wigtown,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (Glasgow): habitational name from Wigton (Cumberland), named with the Old English personal name Wicga + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. It is unclear whether the Scottish name is of independent origin, but it does not seem to be associated with Wigtown (Wigtownshire).

nameMeanings.name: Norfolk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the county of Norfolk in East Anglia, so called from an Old English tribal name composed of the elements north ‘north’ + folc ‘people’ (in contrast to the sūthfolc of Suffolk).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John Leche - Esquire of Eggemere and Wigtown, Norfolk was born in 1255, in Derby, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Edmund Le Leche, was 25 and his mother, Mrs. Margaret Le Leche, was 10. He married Cecilla de Dunbar before 1275. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died after about 1370, in Chester, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Leche - Esquire of Eggemere and Wigtown, Norfolk est né en 1255, à Derby, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Edmund Le Leche, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Margaret Le Leche, avait 10 ans. Il a épousé Cecilla de Dunbar avant 1275. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé après environ 1370, à Chester, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Leche - Esquire of Eggemere and Wigtown, Norfolk 1255 in Derby, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edmund Le Leche 25 und seine Mutter Mrs. Margaret Le Leche 10. Er hat vor 1275 Cecilla de Dunbar geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb nach um 1370 in Chester, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Leche - Esquire of Eggemere and Wigtown, Norfolk è nato il nel 1255 nel Derby, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Edmund Le Leche, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Margaret Le Leche, aveva 10 anni. Lui sposato Cecilla de Dunbar prima del 1275. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il circa 1370, nel Chester, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John Leche - Esquire of Eggemere and Wigtown, Norfolkが1255年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアデルビーで生まれた時，父Edmund Le Lecheは25歳，母Mrs. Margaret Le Lecheは10歳であった。1275年以前にCecilla de Dunbarと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1370年以後にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアチェスターで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Leche - Esquire of Eggemere and Wigtown, Norfolk(이)가 1255년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 더비에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edmund Le Leche(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Margaret Le Leche(은)는 10세였다. 그는 1275년 이전 Cecilla de Dunbar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1370년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 체스터에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Leche - Esquire of Eggemere and Wigtown, Norfolk nasceu em 1255, em Derby, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Edmund Le Leche, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Margaret Le Leche, tinha 10 anos. Ele casou-se com Cecilla de Dunbar antes 1275. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu após aproximadamente 1370, em Chester, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Leche - Esquire of Eggemere and Wigtown, Norfolk родился в 1255 г. в местности дирби, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Edmund Le Leche было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Margaret Le Leche, 10. Он женился на женщине по имени Cecilla de Dunbar перед 1275 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер после около 1370 г. в местности честир, чешир, Англия, Великобритания.
